title: Pigea monopetala
taxonomy: Pigea monopetala is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. This family is a group of evergreen trees and shrubs commonly known as the memecyclid or loganioid clade. Melastomataceae is part of the order Myrtales, which is a larger group of flowering plants. The order Myrtales is part of the clade rosids, one of the two main groups of flowering plants. The clade rosids is a subgroup of the larger clade angiosperms, the flowering plants.
uses: Pigea monopetala is used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world, particularly in Africa and Asia. The health benefits of Pigea monopetala include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which have been used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin infections.
